data.title: DuPont (DD) Raises Its Dividend a Staggering 200%
data.description:
DuPont de Nemours (DD), a maker of construction materials, has raised its quarterly dividend payment an astounding 200% as its financial results recover after years of underperformance.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

Delaware-based DuPont, which makes everything from household insulation to quartz countertops, has declared a quarterly distribution to its stockholders of $0.60 per share, a 200% increase from its previous dividend of $0.20 a share.

The new dividend gives DD stock a yield of 1.74%. The next dividend will be paid on September 15 to shareholders of record as of August 31 this year. DuPont has paid an uninterrupted quarterly dividend for more than a century, going all the way back to 1904. The company has raised its dividend each year since 2019 following the conclusion of a restructuring at DuPont.

DuPont Turns a Corner

After years of underperformance, DuPont underwent a major restructuring over the last decade designed to streamline its business operations. The company spun off its electronics business unit and its fiber business that was home to the iconic Kevlar brand. Today, DuPont focuses on technologies aimed at the healthcare sector and water purification.

The result has been that Dupont’s financial results have improved. The company most recently reported strong first quarter 2026 results, posting sales of $1.68 billion, a 4% increase year-over-year, and earnings per share (EPS) of $0.55, both of which beat Wall Street’s consensus forecasts.

In a statement, management said the improving financials have enabled DuPont to raise its dividend as much as 200%. While the divided increase of 200% is unusual, it is not unheard of for companies to raise their distribution by such big amounts. Earlier this year, Nvidia (NVDA) increased its dividend by 2,400% from $0.01 to $0.25 per share.

Is DD Stock a Buy?

DuPont’s stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among nine Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on six Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average DD price target of $102.33 implies 25% downside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: IBM Is Turning Its Boring AI Pitch into a Real Strategy
data.description:
International Business Machines (IBM) is turning what was once viewed as a boring artificial intelligence (AI) pitch into a strategy that is starting to look increasingly valuable. The global technology and consulting company has never been the flashiest name in AI. It does not build AI models that grab headlines or chase consumer trends. Instead, IBM helps large enterprises make AI work within their existing systems. This focus is becoming increasingly relevant as businesses move from experimenting with AI to deploying it.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

Explore IBX for 2X leverage on IBM

I am bullish on IBM. Software is growing fast, the AI consulting backlog is building, and free cash flow just hit its best Q1 level in a decade. For a stock trading below its own historical valuation, that combination is worth a serious look.
data.author: Hillary Remy

data.title: This Grocery Store Chain Just Raised Its Dividend 11%
data.description:
Kroger (KR), a leading U.S. grocery store chain, has raised its quarterly dividend by 11%.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Management at Kroger declared a quarterly dividend payment of $0.39 per share, an 11% increase from the previous shareholder distribution of $0.35. The new dividend is payable on Sept. 1 of this year to shareholders of record as of Aug. 15.

The new quarterly dividend gives KR stock a forward yield of 2.65%. Kroger has now raised its dividend for 20 consecutive years. The company has consistently paid a distribution to shareholders since 2006. An earlier dividend from Kroger was suspended in the early 2000s as the company underwent a restructuring.

Kroger’s New CEO

Earlier this year, Kroger named former Walmart (WMT) executive Greg Foran as its new chief executive officer (CEO). Kroger had been searching for a new CEO for more than a year after former CEO Rodney McMullen was ousted over an undisclosed personal conduct issue.

Foran was previously the CEO of Walmart’s U.S. operations. Kroger is currently the third largest grocery store chain in the U.S. as measured by sales volumes. It sits behind Walmart and Costco Wholesale (COST), which are the largest grocery store chains in America.

Kroger had tried to grow and gain market share through a proposed merger with rival grocery store chain Albertsons (ACI). However, the companies called off the proposed $25 billion merger when it faced increased regulatory scrutiny and appeared unlikely to be approved in the U.S.

Is KR Stock a Buy?
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: JPM, GS, WFC: U.S. Banks Raise Dividends 10% or More After Passing Annual Stress Test
data.description:
Leading U.S. banks have raised their quarterly dividends by 10% or more after passing the U.S. Federal Reserve’s annual stress test.

TipRanks' Prime Week Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

JPMorgan Chase (JPM), the largest U.S. commercial bank, announced a new $50 billion stock buyback program and raised its dividend by 10%. At the same time, Goldman Sachs (GS) and Wells Fargo (WFC) each announced that they are increasing their dividends by 11%.

Morgan Stanley (MS) raised its dividend 15% and announced a new $20 billion share repurchase program. Bank of America (BAC) said in a statement that it will make an announcement on its dividend next month, but it is also expected to increase its quarterly distribution to shareholders.

The Fed’s Stress Test

The Federal Reserve’s annual stress test found that all 32 large U.S. banks remained above their minimum capital requirements. This year’s stress test ran banks through a hypothetical recession that generated more than $700 billion in projected losses across the financial industry.

The annual stress test was implemented by the U.S. central bank following the 2008 financial crisis and is meant to ensure that America’s largest banks have the capital and risk controls needed to withstand a major crisis and remain solvent. Banks typically wait until after they pass the stress test each year to announce shareholder rewards such as dividends and stock buybacks.

Is BAC Stock a Buy?

Bank of America’s stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 17 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 14 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average BAC price target of $61.75 implies 6% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 3 Dividend Aristocrat Stocks with 20%+ Upside — June 25, 2026
data.description:
Abbott Laboratories (ABT), Cintas (CTAS), and McDonald’s (MCD) are three dividend aristocrat stocks offering more than 20% upside potential, according to the TipRanks Dividend Aristocrat tool.

TipRanks' Prime Week Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Dividend aristocrats are companies listed on the flagship S&P 500 index (SPX) that have raised their dividends every single year for at least 25 years. These favor these stocks for both steady income and growth potential.

Why These 3 Dividend Aristocrat Stocks Stand Out

The three stocks above have a dividend payout ratio above 70%, meaning that the companies recently distributed more than two-thirds of their net earnings to shareholders as dividends.

Be sure to click on any ticker to thoroughly research the stock before you decide whether to add it to your portfolio. Here are today’s top dividend aristocrat stock picks from TipRanks.

  1. Abbott Laboratories (ABT) — This is a 138-year-old American healthcare company that specializes in medical devices and diagnostic instruments. It also sells branded medications. The Illinois-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 137.39%. Abbott paid 51 cents a share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 2.70%. ABT stock currently boasts a Strong Buy consensus rating from 20 Wall Street analysts. This comes with an average price target of $117.75, suggesting more than 26% upside.
  2. Cintas (CTAS) — This is a 97-year-old U.S. specialty business services company that is the largest uniform supplier in North America. The company also provides facility services such as restroom supplies, first aid, and workplace safety products, as well as fire protection services. The Ohio-based firm currently has a dividend payout ratio of 75.95%. Cintas paid 45 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 1.05%. Its shares currently carry a Moderate Buy consensus rating from 10 analysts on Wall Street. This comes with an average price target of $212.44, which implies approximately 26% upside.
  3. McDonald’s (MCD) — This American fast food giant is the company behind the prominent McDonald’s restaurant franchise. The Chicago-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 117.50%. McDonald’s paid $1.86 per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 2.68%. Its shares currently carry a Moderate Buy consensus rating from 23 Wall Street analysts. This comes with an average price target of $329.80, which suggests about 24% upside.

More on Dividend Aristocrats

Some observers also treat recent spin-offs as dividend aristocrats if their parent companies meet the required track record, effectively granting the offshoot the parent’s dividend history.

Interested in More Dividend Aristocrat Stocks?

To find more stocks like these, take a look at TipRanks’ Dividend Aristocrats tool.

The page provides an exhaustive list of all dividend aristocrats, including their most recent dividend yields, previous dividend amounts, payout ratios, and other details.

data.title: This Wall Street Titan Just Raised Its Dividend 15% and Announced a $20 Billion Stock Buyback

TipRanks' Prime Week Sale - 70% Off

The increased shareholder rewards come after Morgan Stanley passed the U.S. Federal Reserve’s annual bank stress test. Going forward, Morgan Stanley will pay a quarterly dividend to its shareholders of $1.15 per share, up from $1 previously.

The new distribution will give MS stock a yield approaching 2%. Morgan Stanley has paid a dividend since 1986 and has raised its distribution for 13 consecutive years. Like all U.S. banks, Morgan Stanley paused its dividend payouts in the years immediately following the 2008 financial crisis.

Other Banks Also Hike Dividends

Morgan Stanley’s latest dividend increase comes after the Federal Reserve’s annual stress test, which found that all 32 large U.S. banks remained above their minimum capital requirements. This year’s test ran banks through a hypothetical recession that generated more than $700 billion in projected losses.

Morgan Stanley isn’t the only major U.S. bank to raise its dividend after the stress test. JPMorgan Chase (JPM) lifted its quarterly payout by 10%, while Goldman Sachs (GS) and Wells Fargo (WFC) each announced that they are increasing their dividends by 11%. Bank of America (BAC) said it will make an announcement on its dividend in July.

Is MS Stock a Buy?

The stock of Morgan Stanley has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 13 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on seven Buy and six Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average MS price target of $204.64 implies 10% downside from current levels.

data.title: 12%-Plus Dividend Yield and Double Digit Upside: Top Analyst Suggests 2 Dividend Stocks to Buy
data.description:
Income investors have long favored high-yield dividend stocks because they provide a steady stream of generous cash income. At the same time, if the underlying business performs well, investors may also benefit from share-price appreciation, creating an additional source of return.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

For investors seeking that combination of income and upside potential, real estate investment trusts (REITs) remain a sector worth exploring. REITs, which operate through the ownership, management, and leasing of various types of real properties and mortgage investments, receive favorable tax treatment in exchange for distributing a significant portion of their taxable income to shareholders – and dividends are frequently used to comply with those rules.

That brings us to mortgage REITs, or mREITs, which invest primarily in mortgage-related assets rather than physical properties. The group has faced significant headwinds in recent years as higher interest rates and weakness in commercial real estate have weighed on sentiment. However, those same pressures may have created an opportunity. As conditions begin to stabilize and valuations remain depressed, some analysts see the potential for both attractive income and meaningful capital appreciation.

Among them is top BTIG analyst Douglas Harter, who believes the risk-reward setup in select mREITs is becoming compelling.

“The residential credit-focused mREITs offer a more discounted valuation than the Agency-focused, plus more book value upside potential over time. We expect book values to be relatively stable and see the current valuation discount being a result of lower relative ROEs/dividend yields (partially as a result of higher operating costs acting as a drag) vs. concerns with the asset quality,” says Harter, who is ranked among the top 4% of Wall Street analysts.

Against this backdrop, Harter believes two mREIT stocks stand out, offering dividend yields of 12% or better along with at least 10% upside potential. We used the TipRanks database to take a closer look at both.

MFA Financial (MFA)

First on today’s stock radar is MFA Financial, a REIT that works as a specialty finance company, using its access to shareholder capital to invest in a range of real estate assets, including residential mortgage loans and mortgage-backed securities (MBSs). MFA is an internally managed REIT and originates residential mortgage loans through its wholly owned subsidiary, Lima One.

The company has a diverse portfolio of assets, leaning heavily on non-QM loans and Agency MBSs, but also including single-family rental loans and residential transitional loans. In addition, the company’s portfolio still includes various older legacy loans. MFA finished last year with its portfolio valued at $12.3 billion; at the end of 1Q26, this total valuation had grown to $12.5 billion. The non-QM and Agency MBS segments make up $5.5 billion and $3.5 billion of the portfolio, respectively.

In Q1, MFA realized net interest income, on all of its investments, of $59.19 million. This figure missed expectations by $1.85 million, even as it grew by nearly 3% year-over-year. At the bottom line, the company’s results were mixed. MFA’s distributable earnings, a key metric that measures the earnings available to cover the dividend, were reported as $31.1 million, which came to 30 cents per common share. Along with this, the company reported distributable earnings prior to realized credit losses of $35.5 million, or 34 cents per share. We should note here that MFA’s regular cash dividend was last declared at 36 cents per common share.

The last dividend payment was made on April 30, pursuant to the March 5 declaration. The next payment, also of $0.36 per share, was declared on June 11 and is scheduled for July 31. The annualized dividend rate of $1.44 per common share translates to a robust forward yield of 15.4%. However, MFA currently faces a dividend coverage gap, as it is paying out more in dividends than it is generating in distributable earnings.

BTIG’s Harter acknowledges that MFA has recently under-earned its dividend from a distributable earnings perspective. However, he views the shortfall as temporary, arguing that the resolution of legacy residential transition loans should improve earnings and allow the dividend to be fully covered by the end of 2026.

“MFA has under-earned its dividend from a DE perspective over the past 5 quarters given the drag from the legacy residential transition loans (both realized losses and unproductive capital). The realized losses are likely to accelerate in 2Q as loan resolutions accelerate before moderating. While the realized losses hit DE they are noneconomic as the assets have already impacted book value through mark to market. Resolving the loans and redeploying the capital should allow for DE to improve and cover the dividend by the end of 2026. Along these lines MFA introduced DE excluding losses last quarter (similar to the way commercial mREITs report) to show the underlying earnings power of the portfolio,” Harter noted.

Quantifying his stance, Harter puts a Buy rating on MFA shares, and his $10.50 price target implies a one-year upside potential of 10%. Add in the dividend yield, and the total one-year return here can reach about 25%. (To watch Harter’s track record, click here)

Overall, there are currently 4 analyst ratings on record for MFA, and the even split of Buy vs. Hold gives the stock its Moderate Buy consensus rating. The shares are currently trading for $9.53 and have an average target price of $10.50, matching the BTIG view. (See MFA stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: This Leading Airline Just Raised Its Dividend 15%
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (DAL), a leading U.S. carrier, has raised its quarterly dividend by 15%.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Atlanta, Georgia-based Delta Air Lines has declared a quarterly distribution of $0.215 per share. That’s up 14.7% from its previous dividend of $0.188. DAL stock now has a dividend yield of 1.02%. The next dividend is payable on July 30 of this year to shareholders of record as of July 9.

This is the third consecutive year that Delta has raised its dividend. The airline has paid a dividend since 2013. However, like most airlines, Delta suspended its quarterly distribution in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic before reinstating the dividend in 2024.

Delta’s Strong Outlook

The 15% dividend increase from Delta Air Lines comes despite the carrier grappling with higher jet fuel costs in recent months as the Iran war raised crude oil prices above $100 a barrel. Despite the cost increase, Delta’s management team as maintained a strong outlook for the carrier this year.

Delta increased its checked baggage fee to help offset soaring jet fuel costs. Additionally, Delta owns a refinery where it turns crude oil into jet fuel, giving it an advantage over other U.S. airlines. Executives at Delta have forecast per-share earnings of $1.50 for the current second quarter, compared with the $1.41 a share that Wall Street had expected from the carrier.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 2 High-Yield Dividend Stocks Scoring a ‘Perfect 10’
data.description:
Investors are constantly searching for an edge — some clue that separates tomorrow’s winners from the rest of the market. One signal can be useful, but when multiple indicators all point in the same direction, the case becomes much harder to ignore. That’s exactly what the TipRanks Smart Score is designed to identify: stocks that excel across a broad range of factors and stand out as potential outperformers.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The Smart Score analyzes vast amounts of market data generated by millions of daily transactions and compares every publicly traded company against a set of factors historically associated with strong future returns. The result is a simple 1-to-10 rating system, with only the highest-ranked stocks earning the coveted “Perfect 10” score.

Of course, a top-rated stock becomes even more compelling when it also pays investors generously. High-yield dividend stocks already offer an attractive combination of income and stability, but when those same names also receive a Perfect 10 Smart Score, it suggests that several powerful signals are working together. For income-focused investors, that’s a combination worth paying attention to.

With that in mind, let’s take a look at two high-yield dividend stocks that have earned a Perfect 10 Smart Score. Using data from the TipRanks platform, we examined the latest metrics and what Wall Street analysts currently have to say about each name.

Ellington Financial (EFC)

We will start our look at ‘Perfect 10’ stocks with high-yield dividends in the world of REITs. These companies, real estate investment trusts, are well-known among dividend investors as champions. REITs are required to return a high percentage of their profits directly to shareholders – they receive favorable tax treatment – and dividends make an easy mode of compliance. Ellington Financial, the first stock on today’s radar, invests in mortgage loans and securities, and along with originating and/or acquiring loans, the company also invests in strategic debt and equity. Ellington’s strategy includes investments in residential and commercial MBSs, and also the securitization of reverse mortgage loans.

The company is externally managed by the larger Ellington Management Group, a major asset manager. The management firm has over $22 billion in AUM, and provides solid backing for the REIT.

Ellington Financial’s portfolio is designed to bring returns for stockholders, and is based on an opportunistic strategy. The company targets investments that are remunerative, and uses diversification to provide a hedge against hard times. The overall approach has built up a portfolio in which the various strengths of the investments complement each other.

That’s been a successful approach. In 1Q26, Ellington generated a total of $149.5 million in investment income, edging past the forecast by $41,000, and saw adjusted distributable earnings of $66.5 million, or $0.55 per common share. That final figure, which was 6 cents per share better than had been expected, is of key importance for dividend investors – as it directly supports the company’s dividend.

The dividend is worth a second look. Ellington pays it out monthly, rather than quarterly – but that can endear it to investors seeking an income stream for regular use, as most individuals’ expenses are paid monthly. Ellington last declared its dividend on June 8, at a monthly rate of 13 cents per common share, to be paid out on July 31. That monthly dividend annualizes to $1.56 per share, and gives a forward yield of 11.5%. Ellington has been paying out dividends regularly since 2010, and switched from quarterly to the current monthly format in 2019.

Maxim analyst Michael Diana sees several reasons to be optimistic about the stock, including solid dividend coverage, a strong track record of preserving book value, and the potential for attractive total returns.

Diana’s noted $15 price target currently implies a 12-month share appreciation of ~12%; when the 11.5% dividend yield is added in, the combined 22.3% one-year upside is slightly higher than his target. Diana rates EFC shares as a Buy. (To watch Diana’s track record, click here)

“We maintain our $0.39 quarterly dividend estimates for 2026 and introduce 2027 dividend estimates at the same level… EFC is guiding to quarterly core earnings of $0.45, implying over-coverage of the dividend. Our 2Q26 EPS estimate is $0.32, as management expects a $0.13 unrealized markdown of its liabilities… In our view, EFC has a best-in-class track record of preserving BV, especially during downturns and dislocations such as the 2008–2009 financial crisis, the so called ‘Taper Tantrum’ in 2013, and the pandemic… Over the next four quarters, we expect: 1) a dividend yield of 11.5%; and 2) stock price appreciation of 10.5% (to our $15 price target), which would result in a 12-month total return of about 22%,” Diana noted.

With the potential for a 22% total return over the next year, Diana rates EFC shares a Buy (To watch Diana’s track record, click here)

Overall, this REIT has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on the Street, based on an even split of Buys and Holds among the four recent analyst reviews. The shares are trading for $13.44, and the $15 average price target matches the Maxim view. (See EFC stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: This Convenience Store Operator Just Raised Its Dividend 14%
data.description:
Casey’s General Stores (CASY) has increased its quarterly dividend by 14%.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The U.S. convenience store operator declared a distribution of $0.65 per share, up substantially from its previous dividend of $0.57 a share. The new dividend is payable on Aug. 14 to stockholders of record as of Aug. 1.

Casey’s began as a general store in Iowa in 1968 and is today the third-largest convenience store operator in the U.S., with more than 2,900 locations, primarily in the Midwest. The company has raised its quarterly dividend for 27 consecutive years. That makes it a Dividend Aristocrat, a title reserved for company’s that have increased their dividend for 25 straight years or longer.

CASY stock has a dividend yield of 0.29%.

Casey’s Latest Earnings

The dividend increase was announced along with fiscal fourth-quarter earnings per share (EPS) of $4.37, which was well above the $3.31 a share forecast among analysts. Revenue of $4.57 billion beat the Wall Street consensus of $4.33 billion. Sales in the quarter were up 14% year-over-year.

In terms of guidance, Casey’s said that it expects fiscal 2027 same-store sales to increase 2% to 5% with an inside margin above 42%. The company also forecast earnings growth of 8% to 10%. Wall Street had same-store sales growth of 3.9% penciled in for the company. CASY stock has risen 81% in the last 12 months.

Casey’s Dividend Growth

Below is a chart that shows the growth of Casey’s dividend in recent years. As one can see, the distribution has been growing steadily as the company prioritizes returning value to shareholders.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Broadcom (AVGO) Did Not Disappoint. It Just Missed a High Bar
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) did not completely disappoint on Q2 results. It just failed to clear an exceptionally high bar. The Q3 artificial intelligence (AI) semiconductor revenue guidance came in slightly below consensus expectations, triggering a sharp sell-off of more than 20% from the stock’s recent all-time high (ATH) of $495. Yet guidance missing a high bar is not the same as AI demand weakening, and Broadcom’s Q2 earnings confirm that the AI infrastructure supercycle is intact.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

I remain bullish on AVGO because record revenue and free cash flow, along with $30 billion in AI bookings, mean the long-term AI growth outlook remains firmly on track.
data.author: Nurhan Gecgil

data.title: Top Morgan Stanley Analyst Lifts UnitedHealth (UNH) Stock Price Target amid Rising Optimism
data.description:
UnitedHealth (UNH) stock surged 5.2% on Thursday amid growing optimism about the health insurer’s improving prospects. Notably, Bank of America analyst Kevin Fischbeck upgraded his rating on UNH stock to Buy from Hold and raised his price target to $450 from $420, citing improving medical cost trends and supportive near-term data points. Additionally, top Morgan Stanley analyst Erin Wright reaffirmed a Buy rating on UnitedHealth stock and boosted her price target to $453 from $395. Let’s look at the reasons driving Wright’s higher price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Here’s Why Morgan Stanley Analyst Is Bullish on UNH Stock  

Wright noted that stocks of managed care organizations (MCOs) have been trending higher amid indications of softer utilization trends. As of Thursday’s closing, Wright’s revised price target of $453 implies 14.3% upside potential for UNH stock from current levels.

Interestingly, the 5-star analyst expects MCOs’ revenues and costs to benefit from artificial intelligence (AI)-driven efficiencies. Wright contends that the market has not yet priced in the impact of these AI tailwinds into the valuations of MCO stocks. The analyst expects AI tailwinds to drive about a 45% average upside in MCOs’ earnings per share (EPS) as efficiencies scale.

UNH stock has risen 20% year-to-date. The company delivered better-than-expected Q1 results and raised its full-year adjusted earnings guidance, indicating it is better positioned to manage high medical costs. Also, UnitedHealth announced a 5% hike in its quarterly dividend to $2.32 per share. UNH stock currently offers a yield of about 3%.

Is UNH Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Overall, Wall Street is bullish on UnitedHealth’s prospects and has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 19 Buys, three Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average UNH stock price target of $403.09 indicates a modest upside potential of 1.7% from current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
